comparisons.source: Category standard + Open Food Facts data (fat_100g: 22.8g)
comparisons.notes: High fat (22.8%) creates oxidation risk — this limits shelf life despite low moisture. Regular plastic pouch = inferior barrier vs vacuum seal.

comparisons.source: Energy bar category standard + food science estimate
comparisons.notes: Sugar + moderate moisture risk. Vacuum seal helps. Himalayan sourcing adds slight antimicrobial advantage.

comparisons.source: Traditional food standard + food science estimate
comparisons.notes: Paper bag = poor moisture barrier. Monsoon season reduces shelf life to 1-2 months. Low fat = low oxidation risk.

comparisons.source: Food science standard for fried snacks
comparisons.notes: Tin can = excellent barrier but deep-fried snacks go rancid due to high fat even in good packaging.

comparisons.source: Open Food Facts + biscuit category standard (fat_100g: 31.08g)
comparisons.notes: Extremely high fat (31%) = fast oxidation = rancidity risk. Very low moisture is good but fat dominates. Open Food Facts verified.

comparisons.source: Himalayan specialty juice literature — limited shelf life without preservatives
comparisons.notes: High moisture (75%) + moderate sugar = moderate shelf life. Altitude sourcing (>2000m) = lower initial microbial load at harvest.

comparisons.source: FSSAI + nuts category standard
comparisons.notes: Very high fat (50%) but very low moisture (3%) + vacuum seal = reasonable shelf life. Key risk is aflatoxin from improper storage pre-processing.

comparisons.source: FSSAI snack food standard + food science
comparisons.notes: Very low moisture (7%) = excellent shelf life for dry snack. Vacuum sealed pouch prevents moisture ingress.

comparisons.source: Estimated from FSSAI standard — direct HimShakti product reference
comparisons.notes: This is the direct equivalent to HimShakti's HS-WBJ-JCE-01 product. Use this to validate AI predictions for that product.

comparisons.source: FSSAI condiment standard
comparisons.notes: Plastic bottle vs glass = shorter shelf life. High sugar + tamarind acid provides moderate preservation.

comparisons.source: Product label + food science estimate
comparisons.notes: Lower salt than mango pickle. Ginger's natural antimicrobial (gingerol) provides some additional protection.

comparisons.source: Food science estimate — curcumin antimicrobial effect
comparisons.notes: Curcumin in turmeric has documented antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Himalayan wild turmeric has higher curcumin content.

comparisons.source: FSSAI jam/chutney standards + food science estimate
comparisons.notes: High sugar (35%) + boiling creates jam-like preservation. Altitude sourcing (>1500m) reduces initial microbial load.

comparisons.source: Product label (verified via Open Food Facts)
comparisons.notes: Source from Open Food Facts API — salt_100g: 13.6g, fat_100g: 14.87g. One of highest-salt pickles in dataset — excellent preservation.

comparisons.source: FSSAI fruit juice beverage standard + pasteurization science
comparisons.notes: Pasteurization at 85°C + aseptic Tetra Pack = 6 month shelf life. Dramatic difference from cold-pressed version.

comparisons.source: Priya Foods product label + food science standard
comparisons.notes: Very high salt (>13%) — excellent preservation. Capsaicin in green chilli has natural antimicrobial properties.

comparisons.source: FSSAI labelling standard + product label data
comparisons.notes: High salt (>10%) + mustard oil acts as dual preservation system. Standard 1-year shelf life for sealed glass jar at room temperature.

comparisons.source: Product label (verified via Open Food Facts — salt_100g: 10.89g)
comparisons.notes: Direct from Open Food Facts API response. Nutrient data verified.

comparisons.source: Food science estimate — allicin in garlic provides natural antimicrobial
comparisons.notes: Allicin compound in garlic has strong antimicrobial properties — extends shelf life beyond what salt alone predicts.

comparisons.source: FSSAI + product label
comparisons.notes: Lower salt compensated by oil barrier and acidity from vegetables.

comparisons.source: Traditional food estimate — jaggery-based sweet
comparisons.notes: Jaggery (25%) at moderate moisture creates risk of insect infestation and sugar crystallization. Short shelf life without vacuum seal.

comparisons.source: Traditional food estimate
comparisons.notes: Very similar to amaranth ladoo profile. Low fat = lower oxidation risk than millet chips.

comparisons.source: FSSAI fruit concentrate standard
comparisons.notes: Concentrate (low moisture + high sugar) behaves more like a preserve than a juice. This is what HimShakti's Wild Berry Juice Concentrate should align with.

comparisons.source: High Brix syrup standard — 65% sugar + low moisture
comparisons.notes: Very high sugar (65%) + low moisture = excellent shelf life. Botanically rich source from 1800m altitude.

comparisons.source: FSSAI squash standard (65°Brix minimum)
comparisons.notes: Squash = concentrated juice + high sugar. 55% sugar = Brix above threshold for preservation.

comparisons.source: Traditional food standard — hygroscopic puffed rice
comparisons.notes: Puffed rice is extremely hygroscopic — absorbs moisture rapidly from air. Very short shelf life in regular pouch despite low moisture at packing.

comparisons.source: Cold-pressed juice standard — no preservatives, no pasteurisation
comparisons.notes: No heat treatment + very high moisture = shortest possible shelf life. Must be refrigerated. FSSAI requires clear short-life labelling.

comparisons.source: Cold-pressed high-moisture juice standard
comparisons.notes: Lemon's citric acid provides some protection but moisture (90%) overwhelms all other preservation factors.

insights.title: AI Accuracy vs Real-World Data: 14%
insights.description: 4 of 28 product comparisons show AI predictions within 20% of known real-world shelf life values.

insights.title: Best AI Performance: SNACK (59% avg accuracy)
insights.description: The AI model performs best for snack products. This aligns with more available food science training data for this category.

insights.title: Salt-Shelf Life Correlation Confirmed
insights.description: Products with 13.6% salt get 4mo prediction vs 3mo for 3.5% salt — AI correctly models the 1 month difference.

insights.title: Altitude Sourcing Bonus Applied in 31 Pickle Cases
insights.description: The AI correctly identified high-altitude sourcing (>1500m) and applied a shelf life bonus in 31 predictions — confirming this HimShakti differentiator is being captured.
